inventory-balance-snapshots.purpose: Provide read-only snapshot truth for inventory position by site, warehouse, lot, and lineage so KPI, audit, and exception review do not query mutable transaction tables directly.

mes-oee-snapshots.purpose: Provide read-only OEE projection by equipment and shift so performance review, loss analysis, and gate meetings use stable snapshot truth rather than recalculating from mutable execution events.

plant-performance-snapshots.purpose: Provide read-only plant KPI snapshots so lean review, management review, and continuous-improvement boards consume a stable multi-signal projection with preserved lineage.

production-bom-snapshots.purpose: Provide read-only BOM snapshot truth per production order so planning, costing, and traceability review can inspect the released material structure without re-querying mutable planning records.

production-route-snapshots.purpose: Provide read-only route snapshot truth per production order so scheduling, traceability, and execution analysis can inspect the released routing structure without depending on mutable route masters.

customer-care-cases.purpose: Control customer complaint, follow-up, escalation, and closure so service issues remain governed and traceable instead of disappearing into touchpoint notes.

customer-purchase-orders.purpose: Capture customer commitment as governed demand so commercial promise, SO linkage, and downstream delivery remain traceable from the first accepted PO.

quotations.purpose: Control commercial estimation, review, offer release, and conversion so customer demand does not bypass governed quote truth before SO commitment.

quote-effectiveness-reviews.purpose: Capture structured quote win-loss and effectiveness analysis so commercial learning, margin review, and continuous improvement are preserved as governed assessment records instead of ad hoc notes.

sales-orders.purpose: Turn accepted commercial demand into governed revenue-producing execution demand spanning engineering, production release, shipment gate, and financial realization.

ap-invoices.purpose: Control AP three-way match, hold, posting, payment, and close so procurement correction cannot bypass financial truth.

ar-invoices.purpose: Represent customer receivable truth from shipment or billing release through dispute and settlement, without depending on a mixed AP/AR legacy object.

backdate-exceptions.purpose: Control governed posting-date exceptions so backdated financial or logistics adjustments cannot bypass closed-period discipline.

credit-memos.purpose: Represent governed downward financial adjustments so AP and AR corrections remain explicit and auditable instead of hiding inside a mixed invoice object.

debit-memos.purpose: Represent governed upward financial adjustments so AP and AR correction exposure stays explicit, traceable, and separate from mixed invoice truth.

fixed-asset-capitalizations.purpose: Represent capitalization decisions that convert spend into governed asset value so maintenance, finance, and asset master stay aligned.

inventory-valuations.purpose: Provide read-only inventory valuation projection per item, site, date, and valuation method so finance review and period close consume stable valuation truth with preserved lineage.

period-closes.purpose: Control accounting period close and reopen decisions so posting gates remain explicit, auditable, and separable from invoice records.

reconciliation-exceptions.purpose: Control MES-ERP mismatch handling so interface drift is triaged, compensated, and closed instead of patched outside governed integration flow.

customer-returns.purpose: Control return authorization, receipt, evaluation, disposition, and closure so reverse-logistics truth stays linked to quality and finance outcomes.

cycle-count-plans.purpose: Control cycle-count scheduling and closure so stock accuracy is corrected through governed counting instead of ad hoc inventory adjustment.

freight-orders.purpose: Controls freight execution from booking through delivery closure so shipment release, carrier handoff, and proof-of-delivery stay under governed logistics truth.

inventory-items.purpose: Controls release, obsolescence, and inventory-usable truth for the shared item master without duplicating inventory item identity.

inventory-movements.purpose: Controls movement posting, hold, and reversal so inventory truth remains reconcilable under corrections and audit.

shipments.purpose: Control outbound release, dispatch, delivery confirmation, and closure so OTIF and export-release truth stay governed from shipment creation to proof of delivery.

stock-balances.purpose: Provide read-only consolidated stock balance truth by item and warehouse so planning, cycle counting, and valuation can consume stable balance projections without mutating source transactions.

warehouses.purpose: Controls warehouse release, hold, and archive so blocked storage areas cannot silently remain active for receiving, picking, or shipment.

five-s-audits.purpose: Controls 5S audit planning, execution, follow-up, verification, and closure so visual-factory discipline becomes a governed lean loop instead of a score-only snapshot.

incidents.purpose: Control incident reporting, containment, investigation, corrective action, and closure so safety events cannot escape the EHS governance loop.

maintenance-plans.purpose: Controls preventive-maintenance plan approval, release, hold, and retirement so recurring PM cadence stays auditable before work orders are auto-released.

maintenance-work-orders.purpose: Control preventive and corrective maintenance execution so equipment intervention, spare usage, and closeout stay governed instead of being hidden in note-only maintenance history.

permits.purpose: Controls permit submission, approval, activation, suspension, expiry, and closure so hazardous or regulated work cannot proceed outside a governed permit-to-work register.

safety-observations.purpose: Controls safety good-catch and hazard observations through assignment, containment, and closure so frontline EHS risks cannot disappear into note-only reporting.

assets.purpose: Provide a canonical asset identity so finance, maintenance, and customer-installed asset references do not split into separate truths.

compliance-obligations.purpose: Controls obligation due, waiver, and closure so permits, audits, and regulatory actions have a governed compliance anchor.

customers.purpose: Controls customer commercial release eligibility, order acceptance, and evidence retention on the customer master.

employees.purpose: Controls labor qualification, access eligibility, and employee release into production and compliance scopes.

equipment.purpose: Controls equipment qualification, lockout, and usable-machine release before maintenance, calibration, and production execution.

measuring-devices.purpose: Provide a canonical measuring-device contract so calibration release, quarantine, and metrology evidence stop drifting between tool-control naming and legacy gauge terminology.

suppliers.purpose: Controls supplier qualification and spend authorization before purchasing or receiving release.

tools.purpose: Control tool readiness, wear containment, and retirement before a tool can be issued to production, maintenance, or calibration use.

machine-alarms.purpose: Capture machine-originated alarm and fault events with operator acknowledgement, escalation, downtime linkage, and maintenance follow-up without manually inventing machine data.

dispatch-lists.purpose: Controls shopfloor dispatch release, hold, and execution closure so operations cannot start or disappear from queue outside the governed MES release horizon.

ipqc-inspections.purpose: Control in-process quality checks so process escapes trigger hold, reinspection, or NCR before final inspection and shipment release.

job-orders.purpose: Translate confirmed sales demand into governed production intent so materials, routing, and downstream WO dispatch stay synchronized with commercial commitment.

production-operations.purpose: Controls operation-level execution, hold, completion, and scrap so genealogy and in-process quality can anchor to the actual operation step.

production-plans.purpose: Control factory plan review, publication, freeze, and completion so dispatch, capacity, and OTIF commitments all read the same released schedule truth.

work-orders.purpose: Control executable shopfloor release, execution, and quality hold at the operation level so production truth is governed where work is actually performed.

iqc-inspections.purpose: Gate inbound material before stock release so supplier quality, quarantine, and NCR escalation are recorded from a governed incoming inspection object.

purchase-receipt-corrections.purpose: Control governed correction and reversal after receipt, IQC, or invoice impact so partial completion can be reconciled through traceable recovery instead of manual patches.

purchase-receipts.purpose: Control physical receipt, quarantine, acceptance, putaway, and reversal so stock and AP matching cannot outrun receiving truth.

purchase-requisitions.purpose: Authorize buy demand before sourcing or PO release so procurement cannot bypass planning, budget, or gate controls.

supplier-asns.purpose: Control inbound advance shipment notice, discrepancy handling, and receiving readiness so physical receipts cannot outrun supplier intent and dock planning truth.

supplier-purchase-orders.purpose: Authorize governed external spend and supplier commitment so receiving, IQC, and AP matching cannot bypass approved procurement intent.

audit-programs.purpose: Control internal and supplier audit planning, execution, verification, and closure so compliance evidence and systemic findings remain governed from schedule through improvement follow-up.

concessions.purpose: Control temporary acceptance or shipment-release exception decisions so quality deviation from requirement cannot bypass governed approval and traceability.

control-plans.purpose: Govern APQP/FMEA-linked control plans so product and process characteristics, control methods, reaction plans, approvals, revisions, and shop-floor inspection execution stay traceable.

corrective-actions.purpose: Drive verified corrective and preventive action so recurring quality loss is closed through owned tasks, effectiveness review, and audit trace.

deviations.purpose: Control temporary process, product, or documentation deviations so exception handling remains explicit, time-bounded, and auditable instead of disappearing inside generic status notes.

document_control.purpose: Govern version control of QMS controlled documents (Manual, Policy, SOP, WI, Form, Annex, Handbook, JD). Distinct from Engineering Change Control (ECC = plm_change_*) which governs parts/BOMs/items.

fqc-inspections.purpose: Preserve the final-quality-control vocabulary used by operations while resolving it onto the governed outgoing-inspection truth so frontend and AI tooling stop treating FQC as a separate storage authority.

improvement-actions.purpose: Control lean improvement intake, approval, implementation, verification, and closure so kaizen and systemic improvement work produces auditable benefit instead of unmanaged ideas.

msa-studies.purpose: Capture measurement-system-analysis and GRR evidence so acceptance of measurement capability is explicit before relying on data for release, SPC, or capability decisions.

nonconformances.purpose: Contain and disposition quality escapes from incoming, in-process, outgoing, or returned product so no defect signal disappears outside a governed NCR record.

oqc-inspections.purpose: Control outgoing quality release so finished goods cannot pass to shipment without governed final inspection truth.

override-controls.purpose: Govern temporary override, waiver, and release bypass decisions so exceptional shipments or process gates remain explicit, timeboxed, signed, and reviewable.

spc-observations.purpose: Preserve structured statistical-process-control observations so process capability, rule violations, and escalation triggers are governed assessment evidence instead of spreadsheet-only history.

lot-genealogy.purpose: Provide forward and backward genealogy for lot, serial, batch, and production-order relationships so containment, recall, NCR, and customer-return decisions can be traced to source records.
